title: T-042 — exceljs Builder generator: 8 tabs per FA + N+1 products
prompt:
# T-042 — D365 Builder xlsx generator

PRD: 01-NPD-PRD.md §10.2, §10.3, §10.5
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
buildD365Workbook(productCode) generates Buffer of Builder_FA<code>.xlsx with 8 tabs (D365_Data, Formula_Version, Formula_Lines, Route_Headers, Route_Versions, Route_Operations, Route_OpProperties, Resource_Req) covering N+1 products (1 final FA + N intermediate PR products) per §10.3.

## Implementation contract
New packages/d365-builder/src/build-workbook.ts. Reads product + prod_detail + Reference.D365_Constants + d365_import_cache. Uses exceljs to compose 8 sheets per spec. Mapping per §10.5. Pure function returns Buffer. Tests use a fixture FA with known prod_detail and assert sheet count + first row in Formula_Version + N+1 product count.

## Files
- packages/d365-builder/src/build-workbook.ts [create]
- packages/d365-builder/src/sheets/ [create]
- packages/d365-builder/__tests__/build-workbook.test.ts [create]
- packages/d365-builder/fixtures/fa-fixture.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given fixture FA5101 with 2 manufacturing operations Strip+Slice, when buildD365Workbook runs, then resulting Buffer parses to a workbook with exactly 8 sheets named per §10.2
2. Given the same fixture, when inspecting Formula_Version sheet, then it has 3 rows (FA5101 + PR5101A + PR5101F) per N+1 rule
3. Given the same fixture, when inspecting Route_Operations OPERATIONNUMBER, then first op is 10 per §10.3

## Test strategy
- RED: 3 tests parsing the Buffer and asserting structure
- Run 'pnpm --filter d365-builder test'

## Risk red lines
- Do not write file to disk in builder (Buffer only; storage is separate task)
- Do not skip Reference.D365_Constants lookup
- Do not regress generator perf <5 s per FA

pipeline_inputs.description: Pure exceljs Builder generator producing 8-sheet Buffer with N+1 products per FA.
